steps.title: Let Them Build the Center
steps.text: The King's Indian (1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 g6) is the ultimate hypermodern defense. Black lets White build a huge pawn center, then strikes it with ...e5 or ...c5. Famous for producing fierce kingside attacks — Kasparov's weapon of choice.

steps.title: The Classical Main Line
steps.text: After the Classical Main Line, White has a huge center. Black's plan: push ...e5, close the center with ...d4 blockade or ...f5, then launch the kingside pawns. Both sides attack simultaneously on opposite flanks — chess at full throttle.

steps.title: Why It's Hard
steps.text: The King's Indian requires accurate calculation — one wrong pawn move and your kingside caves in. But if you love dynamic imbalances and attacks on the king, there's nothing better.
